evidence_quotes.quote: Currently, many games become unplayable when servers are shut down or DRM (Digital Rights Management) systems are no longer maintained.
Even more troubling is the growing trend of publishers deliberately disabling or removing access to games that have already been sold.
In effect, this practice destroys a product that consumers legally own.
1. Provide an offline or archival mode for games once support ends
Remove DRM restrictions that prevent continued use
Release server code or enable community-hosted alternatives where feasible
Clearly communicate end-of-life timelines at the point of sale
Prohibit the remote disabling or deletion of purchased games.
the requirement for an End-of-Life Plan for digital games.

paragraphs.text: I am writing to advocate for an essential addition to the Digital Fairness Act: the requirement for an End-of-Life Plan for digital games. As digital entertainment becomes increasingly central to European culture and commerce, we must ensure that consumers retain access to the products they purchaseeven after official support ends. Currently, many games become unplayable when servers are shut down or DRM (Digital Rights Management) systems are no longer maintained. This undermines consumer rights and erodes digital heritage. Even more troubling is the growing trend of publishers deliberately disabling or removing access to games that have already been sold. In effect, this practice destroys a product that consumers legally own. Its the digital equivalent of taking a book off someones shelf and burning it. This is not just unfairits unacceptable. I propose that the Digital Fairness Act include provisions requiring publishers to: 1. Provide an offline or archival mode for games once support ends 2. Remove DRM restrictions that prevent continued use 3. Release server code or enable community-hosted alternatives where feasible 4. Clearly communicate end-of-life timelines at the point of sale 4. Prohibit the remote disabling or deletion of purchased games. These measures would protect consumers, preserve artistic and cultural works, and ensure that digital ownership is meaningful. Games are not just productsthey are experiences, stories, and communities. They deserve the same respect and longevity as other forms of media. Thank you for your attention to this matter.
